runtime_target: local-first web or desktop application with a graph-backed research-stage dependency model
minimum_viable_operational_slice: Implement a live seed-question walkthrough with one cross-field transfer and one downstream stale-state propagation.
module_contract: Observatory intake and problem decomposition | Scanner and source-field discovery | Bridge mechanism-transfer mapper | Cross-Pollination and XPol fit-pressure tester | Incubator research-object builder | Forge writing/export gate | stage-dependency stale-state engine
data_contract: Every run must persist input fixture, source role, operation, output artifact, review decision, claim ceiling, and timestamp.
source_bound_gate: Every generated research object must preserve seed question, source-field mechanism, fit-pressure test, rejected analogy, and downstream dependency status.
interaction_contract: A reviewer must be able to follow the work from input to operation to output without relying on summary prose.
test_contract: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output | dependency test: upstream change marks downstream work stale
definition_of_done: One end-to-end fixture runs through the named modules, emits a durable artifact, passes source and claim-ceiling gates, and can be inspected without private paths.
blocked_claims_until_done: Do not claim production readiness, external validation, or general empirical findings until live runs, source close reads, and reviewer captures exist.
